source: 2026 Faculty of Law Handbook; 2026 General Rules and Policies Handbook

llb_three_year_graduate.name: LLB - Three-year graduate stream

children.label: Complete a seminar and research-paper elective
Approved Moot Competition substitution (Moot Caput option)

admission_notes: Admission requires an appropriate completed bachelor's degree and Faculty admission under the Undergraduate Prospectus.
progression_notes: FP7-FP10 regulate how many outstanding courses may be carried into the next level and require timetable compatibility.
The readmission indicator is not a Faculty Examinations Committee or Senate decision.
FP14's 45% final-examination subminimum cannot be verified from an ordinary transcript total alone.
award_notes: FP18 permits Senate to condone a failure and permits exceptional treatment of transferred courses.
Cum laude and magna cum laude remain Senate awards, not automatic software decisions.

llb_two_year_combined.name: LLB - Two-year graduate stream after Law and Humanities/Commerce

children.label: Complete a seminar and research-paper elective
Approved Moot Competition substitution (Moot Caput option)

admission_notes: The six Preliminary Level law courses must already have been completed in the prior BA, BSocSc, BCom or BBusSc route.
Admission to the graduate part is not automatic and remains subject to the Undergraduate Prospectus and Faculty decision.
progression_notes: FP7-FP10 regulate how many outstanding courses may be carried into the next level and require timetable compatibility.
The readmission indicator is not a Faculty Examinations Committee or Senate decision.
FP14's 45% final-examination subminimum cannot be verified from an ordinary transcript total alone.
award_notes: FP18 permits Senate to condone a failure and permits exceptional treatment of transferred courses.
Cum laude and magna cum laude remain Senate awards, not automatic software decisions.
